title: GROUP_SEND passing group_id to broadcast_to_group for offline membership re-check is correct
description: GROUP_SEND passes Some(&group_id) to broadcast_to_group, enabling the membership re-check guard for offline enqueue. This prevents the race where a member departs between list_group_members and the enqueue, receiving a message from a group they've already left. A contributor might suggest removing this guard as 'excessive DB calls' (it's one is_group_member per offline recipient). The guard is necessary: without it, group membership state in offline queues diverges from actual membership. The performance cost is proportional to offline members, which is typically a small fraction of group size.
